mechanism_narrative: A downward move in FEDFUNDS lowers discount rates and raises equity present values, so S&P 500 forward returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when policy is easing. The signal is falsified if rate cuts are not followed by higher index returns than unchanged-rate periods.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not higher than baseline after FEDFUNDS declines over multiple rolling windows, or if the sign is negative in most easing episodes, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and risk appetite, which depresses earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when unemployment increases but equities still rally consistently.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment rises are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances, or if Rank IC is not negative in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in RSAFS and PAYEMS identifies strengthening consumer demand and labor income, which should lift near-term revenue expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is not explained by either series alone but by their joint improvement.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-RSAFS/high-PAYEMS state does not outperform single-series states on 20-day forward returns, or if the interaction term is not positive over rolling walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and improved business activity, which raises cyclical earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal fails if industrial production gains are not followed by short-horizon equity strength.
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly changes in INDPRO are followed by nonpositive 5-day S&P 500 returns more often than positive returns, or if short-horizon Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is elevated while FEDFUNDS remains restrictive, real discount rates and recession risk rise, which should produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The hypothesis is falsified if restrictive policy with high inflation still coincides with broad equity gains.
failure_condition: Reject if the high-inflation/high-policy-rate state does not predict negative 20-day returns, or if the interaction coefficient is indistinguishable from zero in successive rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals weakening residential demand and tighter activity-sensitive growth expectations, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect is strongest when housing weakness persists for several months rather than one isolated print.
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST drawdowns are not followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns on average, or if the sign turns positive across repeated housing downturn episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, improving PAYEMS combined with falling UNRATE indicates a healthier labor market, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through stronger spending and earnings expectations. The signal is invalid if labor-market healing does not translate into broad market gains in recovery phases.
failure_condition: Reject if the combination of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to outperform the opposite state on 60-day forward returns, or if the interaction effect is not positive in recovery windows.

mechanism_narrative: A slowdown in GDPC1 growth following prior expansion signals fading aggregate demand, which should reduce S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The claim fails if decelerating GDP growth does not coincide with weaker equity performance.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns negative versus prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if GDP growth decelerations are not associated with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the sign of the return relationship is positive in most late-cycle episodes.
